items.title: AI Impact: How AI Raised the Bar for Lawyers - Newsweek
items.summary: # AI Impact: How AI Raised the Bar for Lawyers. ## AI Impact examines legal AI workloads, hidden productivity costs, finance transformation and AI-powered health care claims. *Editor's note: This is AI Impact, Newsweek's weekly newsletter where each week, we will explore how business leaders are unlocking real value through artificial intelligence.*. #### Read More on AI. AI is now changing that reputation, though not in the way many lawyers may have expected. Ironclad, an AI contracting platform, found in its third annual “State of AI in Legal” report that 92 percent of legal professionals now use AI in some capacity for legal work, up from 69 percent in 2025. “AI is not just reducing workload; it is changing what legal teams are expected to deliver,” Jasmine Singh, general counsel at Ironclad, told *Newsweek*. Ironclad found that 94 percent of respondents who use AI for legal work use it for contract-specific tasks.

items.title: Acrisure: How AI is the Critical Core Engine for Growth - FinTech Magazine
items.summary: # Acrisure: How AI is the Critical Core Engine for Growth. Benjamin Funk, Chief Technology & AI Officer at Acrisure, explains how the company’s implementation of AI at its core is key to its success. For the modern business, AI is more than just a means to an end. Aside from the front-end benefits focused on efficiency, AI is adopting a vital role in every boardroom: strategy. Simply put, AI is more than a tool in fintech. It is rapidly becoming the solution to every company’s growth strategy. In this Q&A with FinTech Magazine, Benjamin Funk, Chief Technology & AI Officer at Acrisure, explains how Acrisure’s AI strategy is the core of its operation. At Acrisure, I’m now leading technology, innovation, and AI across our engineering, data, analytics, product, sales tech and agentic automation teams. Not as an overlay, but as a critical and core engine for how a business ought to run.

items.title: AI Startup Artisan Accused of Stealing ‘This is Fine’ Comic Art - Startup Ecosystem Canada
items.summary: By Startup Eco SystemNo Comments. ## AI Startup Artisan Accused of Stealing ‘This is Fine’ Comic Art. KC Green, the artist behind the popular ‘This is fine’ meme, has accused AI startup Artisan of using his artwork without permission in an advertisement campaign. The ad, spotted in a subway station, altered Green’s original comic to promote ‘Ava the AI BDR’. Artisan responded by expressing respect for Green and has planned to discuss the matter with him directly. #### ‘This is fine’ creator says AI startup stole his art. acquisition AI AI development AI infrastructure AI innovation AI integration AI investment AI models AI safety AI technology Amazon Anthropic Apple Artificial Intelligence Autonomous vehicles Canada founder news Canada startup news ChatGPT cryptocurrency Cybersecurity Electric Vehicles Elon Musk entrepreneurship fintech Funding round Generative AI Global founder news Global startup news healthcare innovation Innovation investment IPO Meta Microsoft Nvidia Ontario startup news OpenAI Sam Altman Seed funding Silicon Valley TechCrunch Disrupt Tech innovation Tesla Toronto startup news venture capital.

items.title: “Manufacturing Sites are the Core of AI Competition”... Ibex Unveils Industrial Physical AI Strategy at AWC 2026 - 벤처스퀘어
items.summary: # “Manufacturing Sites are the Core of AI Competition”… Ibex Unveils Industrial Physical AI Strategy at AWC 2026. On the 27th, AIVEX gave a presentation on the topic of "Industrial Physical AI Technology Trends and Applications" at the "AI World Congress 2026 (AWC 2026)" held at Ajou University. In this announcement, the company explained that the era of 'Physical AI,' where AI perceives, judges, and acts upon the real physical world—moving beyond Generative and Agentic AI—is beginning in earnest. It explained that AI, which used to understand language and knowledge through text-based learning, is now evolving to a stage where it understands and interacts with the physical environment based on video and behavioral data. Seong Min-soo, CEO of Ibex, stated, “The center of AI is expanding beyond generative AI in the digital space to physical AI operating in actual industrial sites,” adding, “We will lead the advancement of manufacturing AI through AI Vision and AI Robotics technologies optimized for manufacturing environments.”.

items.title: Tesla is recovering in Europe. BYD is still beating it. - Sherwood News
items.summary: Tesla’s recovery across Europe continued last month, but it still lags BYD, according to new data from the European Automobile Manufacturers’ Association. In April, BYD registered more than twice as many cars as Tesla in the EU, UK, and the European Free Trade Association. Tesla thinks upcoming approval of its supervised Full Self-Driving tech is integral to its success on the continent. Tesla recently received approval for a version of its supervised FSD in the Netherlands, its first European market, and expects “EU-wide” approval in the second or third quarter of this year. The companies’ soaring valuations and annualized recurring revenue (ARR) have been running neck and neck for months, and The Information now reports that Anthropic is generating an estimated 35% more revenue than OpenAI. According to The Information’s reporting, Anthropic is close to a staggering $45 billion ARR, while OpenAI is at an estimated $33 billion ARR.

items.title: Tesla Stock Is In A Three-Weeks-Tight, Continues Its Recovery From A Deep Slump - Investor's Business Daily
items.summary: # Tesla Stock Is In A Three-Weeks-Tight, Continues Its Recovery From A Deep Slump. **Tesla** (TSLA) stock started the week in a three-weeks-tight pattern, formed in the handle of a cup-with-handle base. ## Tesla Stock Is Three-Weeks-Tight. Tesla's three-weeks-tight pattern comes after the stock got above the 200-day line earlier this month, according to MarketSurge. This occurs as the stock recovers from an extended slump begun in December. At one point, Tesla stock had finished down eight weeks in a row. Tesla stock is working toward a buy point of 453.40, which is the high point of its current handle, as well as the three-weeks-tight pattern. Investors looking for an early entry could have used the stock's move above the 10-day line on Tuesday. ## Tesla Stock. As a company, Tesla is in the midst of reinventing itself from an automaker to an AI and robotics firm. items.title: Tesla Logs Strong Growth in European Monthly Sales - WSJ
items.summary: # Tesla European Sales Rose 46.5% as BYD Registrations Doubled - WSJ. https://www.wsj.com/business/autos/tesla-logs-strong-growth-in-european-monthly-sales-5fbe07a2. # Tesla Logs Strong Growth in European Monthly Sales. Image 1: People walking past a Tesla Inc. showroom in Stockholm, Sweden. New-car registrations for Tesla models jumped 46.5% on year last month to 10,654 units across the EU, U.K., Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway and Switzerland.Erik Flyg/Bloomberg News. Tesla’sTSLA 1.78%increase; green up pointing triangle sales in Europe grew strongly in April, a sign that Elon Musk’s electric-vehicle maker continues to regain momentum in the region after a slump that stretched more than a year. New-car registrations for Tesla models, a reflection of sales, jumped 46.5% on year last month to 10,654 units across the European Union, the U.K., Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway and Switzerland, according to the European Automobile Manufacturers’ Association, an industry body also known as ACEA. items.title: Tesla Self-Certified Its Model Y Robotaxis for Level 4 Autonomy Under a New Texas Law - autoevolution
items.summary: # Tesla Self-Certified Its Model Y Robotaxis for Level 4 Autonomy Under a New Texas Law. Tesla benefited from a new Texas law that allows companies to self-certify their robotaxis to provide commercial driverless rides across the state. Tesla can now offer paid robotaxi rides across Texas, thanks to a new law that allows companies to self-certify their autonomous driving systems. The Level 4 certification allows Tesla to provide paid rides in Texas with no drivers or operators in the vehicle. This might be unrelated, but the same day Texas authorized Tesla for driverless Robotaxi operations, Elon Musk shared the video of a Cybercab driving off the production line at Giga Texas without a steering wheel. More significantly, Musk's post was retweeted by Tesla AI chief Ashok Elluswamy, who said that autonomous Cybercabs will soon be driving themselves *"to Austin city, reporting for duty."* This suggests that Tesla will soon start offering driverless rides in the Cybercab, in addition to Model Y robotaxis, across Texas. Tesla robotaxi Tesla autonomous driving Texas tesla cybercab.

items.title: Gemini’s new AI agent is about as good as Google’s demo - The Verge
items.summary: # Gemini’s new AI agent is about as good as Google’s demo. Gemini Spark is impressive, but it’s not worth the cost just yet. It also advertises *at the very top* *of the Spark website* that it’s “always under your direction,” that “you choose to turn it on,” and that “it’s designed to check with you before taking major actions.” Given the mounting skepticism toward AI, it’s very much “my ‘not involved in rogue AI’ T-shirt has people asking questions already answered by my shirt.”. For my own version, I asked it to make a calendar event each month ahead of my wife’s birthday and make it hot pink, draft an email to my family about sending them the first episode of the latest season of *Taskmaster*, and create a document with the top things my wife and I need to know about getting our toddler ready for preschool.

items.title: After six years, Google's new Home speaker might actually have a release date - Android Police
items.summary: # After six years, Google's new Home speaker might actually have a release date. We’ve been wondering: Will the Google Home smart speaker ever get a release date? In the US, the Google Home speaker will also be available in the serene green Jade and punchy pink Berry. Google announced the Google Home Speaker in October of last year and gave it a release date of spring 2026. If the Best Buy Canada listing has the correct date, that puts it four days past spring and into summer. The Google Home Speaker is “engineered for Gemini,” and that might be the reason why it hasn’t yet met its targeted release date. There is a lot of pressure for the Google Home Speaker to work smoothly right out of the gate since it’s been so long anticipated and because it will be the first Google smart home product created around Gemini.

items.title: WWCD 2026: Everything Apple is expected to announce on June 8 - Newsweek
items.summary: # WWDC 2026: Everything Apple Is Expected to Announce on June 8. Apple's biggest event of the year lands on Monday, June 8, and for once, the rumor mill has been unusually productive. Apple pre-records the keynote and streams it on Apple.com, YouTube, the Apple TV, and the Apple Developer app, so there's no excuse to miss it. Siri improvements announced at WWDC 2024 were delayed, but a January 2026 partnership with Google, bringing Gemini models into the mix, is what's expected to finally enable the more advanced capabilities. Apple is partnering with Google and plans to use a custom AI model built in collaboration with Google's Gemini team for some of the new Siri features, including the chatbot functionality. Apple is launching its first foldable iPhone this September, and iOS 27 needs to accommodate it - the iPhone Fold will support two apps side-by-side for the first time, with an iPad-like display when open and a standard iPhone layout when closed.

items.title: Dying star resembles a billowing crystal ball in new telescope photo - Los Angeles Times
items.summary: Tap to enable a layout that focuses on the article. # Dying star resembles a billowing crystal ball in new telescope photo. * Click here to listen to this article. The Gemini North Telescope atop Hawaii’s tallest peak, Mauna Kea, captured the star in its last gasps. The image was released by the National Science Foundation’s NOIRLab, which operates the telescope. It’s actually a binary star system 1,500 light-years away, nicknamed the Crystal Ball Nebula because of the milky white, spherical cloud around it. This cloud of gas forms when a star sheds its outer layers near the end of its life. The exposed stellar core heats the cloud to tens of thousands of degrees, giving it an ethereal glow. Gemini North last year observed the nebula — formally known as NGC 1514 — and the image was then completed in color last week. ### In UAE, one of the world’s most light-polluted places, a revelation in the desert: The Milky Way.
